steps.title: Complete the identification
steps.content: The phrase what species it belonged to forms a complete clause. The species name Ochus subvittatus is then introduced as the direct identification of that species.

steps.title: Use a colon for the name
steps.content: A adds and, making the species name an illogical list item. C uses a semicolon before a noun phrase, but a semicolon should separate independent clauses. D leaves the name dangling without punctuation to introduce the identification. The remaining punctuation correctly introduces the species name after the complete clause. The answer is B.
